---
title: "Overview"
---

# Overview

This documentation describes the conception and development of a data
visualisation product created for the module *Visualisation and Data Science
Storytelling II* (ZHAW, FS 2025/26) by Group 11 (Lara Pestrin, Jonathan Kull,
Veprim Mehmeti).

The project — **Grocery Product Placement Analysis** — analyses the Instacart
Online Grocery Shopping Dataset (2017) to understand grocery purchasing
patterns. It explores which products are bought most frequently, which products
are commonly bought together (market basket analysis), and which products show
the highest customer loyalty as measured by reorder rate. The insights are
communicated through an interactive dashboard that supports data-driven
recommendations for product placement and category organisation.

![The visualisation product development process](pics/vizproductprocess.png){#fig-vizprocess}

The methodological procedure follows the process model shown in
@fig-vizprocess. This documentation comprises the following sections:

- **[Project Charta](project_charta.qmd)** — context and scope, stakeholder and
  user analysis (including personas), project objectives, success criteria,
  constraints, and the visualization concept.
- **[Data Report](data_report.qmd)** — the raw and processed datasets, the data
  acquisition and processing steps, and the exploratory data analysis that
  informs the visualisations.

The interactive visualisation product itself is a Streamlit dashboard. See the
project `README` for instructions on how to set up the environment and run it.
